skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, inspect, edit, convert, validate, and thumbnail PowerPoint .pptx files using OOXML workflows and helper scripts.
skill.summary: PowerPoint creation and OOXML editing workflows for AI coding agents.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, network, and heuristic matches. Most weak-crypto, C2, hardcoded URL, and Ruby backtick findings are false positives from Markdown prose, XML schemas, OOXML namespace strings, or JavaScript template literals. However, the skill includes helper scripts that process untrusted Office ZIP archives with extractall, plus local external tool execution, so publication should wait for remediation or clear sandboxing guidance.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Archive Extraction
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The Office unpacking helpers extract ZIP-based Office files with extractall. A crafted .pptx, .docx, or related archive could write files outside the intended output directory unless every member path is validated before extraction.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static finding maps to direct archive extraction calls on user-supplied Office files. This is a well-known Zip Slip risk when member names are not checked before extraction.

medium_findings.title: Local External Tool Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill runs local tools such as LibreOffice, Poppler, and git for conversion, thumbnail generation, validation, and redline comparison. The sampled subprocess calls use argument arrays rather than shell strings, but they still process untrusted document content through large external applications.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The calls are real subprocess invocations and are relevant to document conversion workflows. Injection confidence is lower because sampled commands use fixed executable names and structured argument arrays.

medium_findings.title: Privileged and Global Dependency Installation Guidance
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documentation instructs users to install packages globally and uses sudo apt-get for LibreOffice and Poppler. This is expected for document tooling but increases operational risk for marketplace users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dependency block explicitly includes global package installation and sudo package installation commands. This is not malicious, but it is a real privilege and supply-chain exposure.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Mostly XML Namespace References
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Many network findings point to OOXML schema namespaces and schemaLocation values, not runtime outbound network calls. These strings describe document standards and do not show data exfiltration by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled lines are namespace constants and schema imports. I did not find evidence that these strings are used for outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Is Temporary Directory Selection
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported environment access in the HTML conversion helper reads TMPDIR to choose a temporary directory. I did not find evidence that secrets are read or transmitted from the environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line uses process.env.TMPDIR only as a path default. The static sensitive-environment label is not supported by the sampled context.

low_findings.title: Heuristic Obfuscation and C2 Matches Lack Malicious Context
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The bracket-chain, C2 keyword, network scanner, and weak-crypto alerts appear to come from XML schema syntax, color names, relationship names, Markdown text, or normal OOXML terminology. No prompt-injection text or malicious override instruction was found in the sampled high-signal areas.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled matches have benign document-schema or presentation-design context. Confidence is not absolute because only targeted review was performed across a large file set.

dangerous_patterns.title: zipfile.extractall on Office Archives
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill extracts ZIP-based Office archives directly. Replace extractall with member-by-member extraction that rejects absolute paths, parent traversal, drive prefixes, symlinks, and paths escaping the output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present and handles user-provided document archives. This is the strongest confirmed issue in the audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Document Conversion Through External Applications
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill processes user-controlled presentations with external desktop tools. These commands should run in a sandbox with network disabled and with limits on file size, CPU, memory, and runtime.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Subprocess use is confirmed, and document converters have meaningful attack surface. The sampled calls do not show shell injection.

content.value_statement: Presentation work often requires both design guidance and direct .pptx file editing. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows for creating, inspecting, converting, and validating PowerPoint files.
actual_capabilities: Creates new .pptx presentations from HTML-based slide layouts.
Extracts and edits raw OOXML for slides, notes, comments, themes, and layouts.
Converts presentations to markdown, PDF, images, or thumbnails using local tools.
Validates presentation structure and common OOXML relationship issues.
Supports replacement workflows for slide text and selected presentation assets.
limitations: Requires local dependencies such as LibreOffice, Poppler, Playwright, and pptxgenjs for some workflows.
Uses helper scripts that need filesystem access to unpack and repack Office archives.
Does not guarantee pixel-perfect fidelity for every PowerPoint feature or animation.
Should process untrusted presentations only in a sandbox until archive extraction is hardened.

use_cases.title: Build a New Slide Deck
use_cases.description: Create a polished PowerPoint from an outline, with consistent typography, colors, layout, and speaker notes.

use_cases.title: Audit an Existing Presentation
use_cases.description: Inspect slides, comments, notes, theme files, and layout XML before making targeted improvements.

use_cases.title: Automate Presentation Maintenance
use_cases.description: Use scripts to unpack, validate, replace content, generate thumbnails, and repack .pptx files.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Presentation Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pptx skill to read this presentation and summarize each slide. Include speaker notes if available.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pptx skill to create a 10-slide presentation about [topic]. Choose a fitting color palette, explain the design direction, and generate the .pptx file.

prompt_templates.title: Edit Existing Slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pptx skill to update the attached presentation. Replace outdated metrics, keep the original theme, and preserve slide layouts.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Repair OOXML
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pptx skill to unpack this .pptx, inspect relationships and slide XML, fix structural issues, validate the result, and create thumbnails for review.

output_examples.input: Create a six-slide quarterly business review deck from this outline.
output: A PowerPoint deck with title, agenda, performance, risks, roadmap, and closing slides.
A short design rationale covering palette, typography, and layout choices.
Speaker notes for each slide when the outline provides talking points.

output_examples.input: Analyze this .pptx and list what needs cleanup before an executive review.
output: A slide-by-slide summary of content and visible issues.
A list of inconsistent fonts, colors, layouts, notes, and comments found during inspection.
Recommended edits ordered by impact and effort.

output_examples.input: Generate thumbnails for every slide after editing the presentation.
output: A set of slide preview images for visual review.
A brief validation note covering conversion success and any rendering issues.

best_practices: Run helper scripts in a temporary workspace and keep original presentations unchanged until validation passes.
Inspect theme, master, layout, slide, notes, and relationship files before complex OOXML edits.
Use a sandbox when processing presentations from unknown or external sources.
anti_patterns: Do not run the unpacking workflow on untrusted files without path traversal protections.
Do not install global dependencies or sudo packages without reviewing the environment policy.
Do not edit XML relationship files without validating the final .pptx package.

faq.question: Can this skill create PowerPoint files from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides agents through HTML-to-PPTX creation workflows and presentation design choices.

faq.question: Can it edit existing .pptx files?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to unpack OOXML, modify slides, notes, comments, layouts, and repack the file.

faq.question: Does it require extra software?
faq.answer: Some workflows require local tools such as LibreOffice, Poppler, Playwright, pptxgenjs, and markitdown.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted presentations?
faq.answer: Use a sandbox. The audit found unsafe archive extraction patterns that should be fixed before broad publication.

faq.question: Can it preserve the original design?
faq.answer: It can inspect theme and layout XML to preserve styles, but complex animations and effects may need manual review.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflows are suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when the required tools are available.
